en: On the third day there was a wedding at Cana in Galilee, and the mother of Jesus was there.
zh: 第三天，在加利利的迦拿有婚筵，耶穌的母親在那裡；

en: Jesus also was invited to the wedding with his disciples.
zh: 耶穌和門徒也被邀請參加婚筵。

en: When the wine ran out, the mother of Jesus said to him, "They have no wine."
zh: 酒用盡了，耶穌的母親對他說：“他們沒有酒了。”

en: And Jesus said to her, "Woman, what does this have to do with me? My hour has not yet come."
zh: 耶穌說：“母親 ，我跟你有甚麼關係呢？我的時候還沒有到。”

en: His mother said to the servants, "Do whatever he tells you."
zh: 他母親告訴僕人說：“他吩咐你們甚麼，就作甚麼。”

en: Now there were six stone water jars there for the Jewish rites of purification, each holding twenty or thirty gallons.
zh: 在那裡有六口石缸，每口可盛兩三桶水，是為猶太人行潔淨禮用的。

en: Jesus said to the servants, "Fill the jars with water." And they filled them up to the brim.
zh: 耶穌吩咐僕人：“把缸都倒滿水！”他們就倒滿了，直到缸口。

en: And he said to them, "Now draw some out and take it to the master of the feast." So they took it.
zh: 耶穌又吩咐他們：“現在舀出來，送給筵席的總管！”他們就送去了。

en: When the master of the feast tasted the water now become wine, and did not know where it came from (though the servants who had drawn the water knew), the master of the feast called the bridegroom
zh: 總管嘗了那水變的酒，不知道是從哪裡來的，只有舀水的僕人知道。總管就叫新郎來，

en: and said to him, "Everyone serves the good wine first, and when people have drunk freely, then the poor wine. But you have kept the good wine until now."
zh: 對他說：“人人都是先擺上好酒，等到親友喝夠了，才擺上次等的，你倒把好酒留到現在。”

en: This, the first of his signs, Jesus did at Cana in Galilee, and manifested his glory. And his disciples believed in him.
zh: 這是耶穌所行的第一件神蹟，是在加利利的迦拿行的。他顯出了自己的榮耀，他的門徒就信了他。

en: After this he went down to Capernaum, with his mother and his brothers and his disciples, and they stayed there for a few days.
zh: 這事以後，耶穌和母親、弟弟、門徒，都下到迦百農去，在那裡住了沒有幾天。

en: The Passover of the Jews was at hand, and Jesus went up to Jerusalem.
zh: 猶太人的逾越節近了，耶穌就上耶路撒冷去。

en: In the temple he found those who were selling oxen and sheep and pigeons, and the money-changers sitting there.
zh: 他在聖殿的外院裡看見有賣牛羊鴿子的，和坐在那裡兌換銀錢的，

en: And making a whip of cords, he drove them all out of the temple, with the sheep and oxen. And he poured out the coins of the money-changers and overturned their tables.
zh: 就用繩索做了一條鞭子，把眾人連牛帶羊都從外院趕出去，倒掉兌換銀錢的人的錢，推翻他們的桌子；

en: And he told those who sold the pigeons, "Take these things away; do not make my Father's house a house of trade."
zh: 又對賣鴿子的說：“把這些東西搬出去，不要把我父的殿當作巿場。”

en: His disciples remembered that it was written, "Zeal for your house will consume me."
zh: 他的門徒就想起經上記著：“我為你的殿心中迫切，如同火燒。”

en: So the Jews said to him, "What sign do you show us for doing these things?"
zh: 猶太人就問他：“你可以顯甚麼神蹟給我們看，證明你有權作這些事呢？”

en: Jesus answered them, "Destroy this temple, and in three days I will raise it up."
zh: 耶穌回答：“你們拆毀這殿，我三天之內要把它建造起來。”

en: The Jews then said, "It has taken forty-six years to build this temple, and will you raise it up in three days?"
zh: 猶太人說：“這殿建了四十六年，你三天之內就能把它建造起來嗎？”

en: But he was speaking about the temple of his body.
zh: 但耶穌所說的殿，就是他的身體。

en: When therefore he was raised from the dead, his disciples remembered that he had said this, and they believed the Scripture and the word that Jesus had spoken.
zh: 所以當耶穌從死人中復活以後，門徒想起了他說過這話，就信了聖經和耶穌所說的話。

en: Now when he was in Jerusalem at the Passover Feast, many believed in his name when they saw the signs that he was doing.
zh: 耶穌在耶路撒冷過逾越節的時候，許多人看見他所行的神蹟，就信了他的名。

en: But Jesus on his part did not entrust himself to them, because he knew all people
zh: 耶穌卻不信任他們，因為他知道所有的人，

en: and needed no one to bear witness about man, for he himself knew what was in man.
zh: 也不需要誰指證人是怎樣的，因為他知道人心裡存的是甚麼。
